title: Allergen reduction device
abstract: An allergen reduction device may include a body, a dust cup removably coupled to the body, a plurality of agitators rotatably coupled to the body and disposed within respective agitator cavities defined in the body, a suction motor disposed within the body and configured to cause air to flow along corresponding cleaning airflow paths from the agitator cavities into the dust cup, a hot air outlet fluidly coupled to the suction motor, the suction motor being configured to urge the air through the hot air outlet along a heated exhaust airflow path, and a heater positioned within the heated exhaust airflow path between the suction motor and the hot air outlet, the heater being configured to heat the air that is urged from the hot air outlet.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. An allergen reduction device comprising: a body; a dust cup removably coupled to the body; a plurality of agitators rotatably coupled to the body and disposed within respective agitator cavities defined in the body; a suction motor disposed within the body and configured to cause cleaning air to flow along corresponding cleaning airflow paths from the agitator cavities into the dust cup; a hot air outlet fluidly coupled to the suction motor, the suction motor being configured to urge the cleaning air through the hot air outlet along a heated exhaust airflow path; and a heater positioned within the heated exhaust airflow path between the suction motor and the hot air outlet, the heater being configured to heat the cleaning air that is urged from the hot air outlet. 2. The allergen reduction device of claim 1, wherein the hot air outlet is disposed between the plurality of agitators. 3. The allergen reduction device of claim 1, wherein the agitators are counter rotating. 4. The allergen reduction device of claim 1, wherein a filter extends within the dust cup. 5. The allergen reduction device of claim 4, wherein the filter is a high efficiency particulate air (HEPA) filter. 6. The allergen reduction device of claim 5, wherein the filter is a glass fabric HEPA filter. 7. The allergen reduction device of claim 1, wherein the suction motor is further configured to cause cooling air to flow along a cooling airflow path, wherein the cooling airflow path extends through the suction motor. 8. The allergen reduction device of claim 7, wherein the cooling airflow path is separate from the cleaning airflow paths and the heated exhaust airflow path. 9. The allergen reduction device of claim 1 further comprising an agitator motor configured to cause the plurality of agitators to rotate. 10. The allergen reduction device of claim 1, wherein each of the agitators include one or more flaps. 11. The allergen reduction device of claim 1, wherein the heater is configured to heat the cleaning air passing therethrough to a temperature in a range of 100 degrees Celsius to 140 degrees Celsius. 12. The allergen reduction device of claim 1, wherein the heater is disposed within a hot air tunnel. 13. The allergen reduction device of claim 12, wherein the hot air tunnel has a cross-sectional area measuring in a range of 400 square millimeters to 2000 square millimeters. 14. An allergen reduction device comprising: a body; a plurality of agitators rotatably coupled to the body and disposed within respective agitator cavities defined in the body; a suction motor disposed within the body and configured to cause air to flow along a cleaning airflow path that extends through at least one of the agitator cavities; a hot air outlet fluidly coupled to the suction motor, the suction motor being configured to urge the air through the hot air outlet along a heated exhaust airflow path; and a heater positioned within the heated exhaust airflow path between the suction motor and the hot air outlet, the heater being configured to heat the air that is urged from the hot air outlet. 15. The allergen reduction device of claim 14, wherein the hot air outlet is disposed between the plurality of agitators. 16. The allergen reduction device of claim 14, wherein the agitators are counter rotating. 17. The allergen reduction device of claim 14, wherein each of the agitators include one or more flaps. 18. The allergen reduction device of claim 14, wherein the heater is configured to heat the air passing therethrough to a temperature in a range of 100 degrees Celsius to 140 degrees Celsius. 19. The allergen reduction device of claim 14, wherein the heater is disposed within a hot air tunnel. 20. The allergen reduction device of claim 19, wherein the hot air tunnel has a cross-sectional area measuring in a range of 400 square millimeters to 2000 square millimeters.
